pi_items.source: Task setup script (setup_task.sh) assigns TARGET_REPO='example-repo-local' and prepares the system to expect this default repository. Export script (export_result.sh) directly queries this specific repository in Artifactory API.
Task setup script (setup_task.sh) sets IMPORT_ROOT='/home/ga/repo-import-data', creates the directory, and copies the downloaded Maven artifacts into it.

Task setup script (setup_task.sh) builds this exact folder structure to mimic a Maven repository and copies the jar and pom files for commons-lang3 and commons-io. Export script (export_result.sh) specifically checks for these four exact paths via the Artifactory API.
Environment setup script (setup_artifactory.sh) sets ADMIN_USER='admin' and configures the Artifactory instance with it. Used in all subsequent API calls in scripts.
Environment setup script (setup_artifactory.sh) sets ADMIN_PASS='password' and completes the first-time initialization by explicitly setting this password via the Artifactory API.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on real Apache Commons jar and pom files downloaded directly from Maven Central. These artifacts are placed in '/home/ga/repo-import-data' following a standard Maven directory structure. The target repository for import is the default 'example-repo-local'. The Artifactory Administration UI is accessible using the configured credentials 'admin' and 'password'.
